meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Solving Version Control for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop losing work to poor versioning. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship stable, version-controlled apps faster.
content.answer_block: To eliminate the chaos of manual file management, adopt a production-grade SaaS starter kit. By integrating Git-based version control into your workflow via Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform disorganized codebases into structured, scalable repositories, ensuring every client deliverable is tracked, stable, and ready for immediate deployment.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: it's 2 AM, the client is asking for a minor tweak, and you realize you've overwritten the 'final-v2' file with a buggy hotfix. Without robust version control, your codebase is a ticking time bomb, and your reputation is on the line. The lack of a structured environment doesn't just kill productivity; it erodes the professional trust you’ve spent years building.

But the era of manual file management is over. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you can bake in version control from line one. We aren't just writing code; we are building an assembly line that handles the heavy lifting of repository management for you. Let's turn that messy project folder into a professional-grade SaaS engine.

steps.title: Initialize the Starter Kit
steps.description: Clone a battle-tested SaaS starter kit to ensure your project structure, authentication, and database schemas are pre-configured for Git tracking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new SaaS project with Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure the directory is initialized with a robust .gitignore file and a standard Git structure.

steps.title: Configure Version Control Hooks
steps.description: Automate your commit quality by setting up pre-commit hooks that validate your code before you ever push to a remote repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure husky and lint-staged to run typescript checks and prettier formatting on every commit. Ensure the config is modular.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "npm install husky lint-staged --save-dev && npx husky install"

steps.title: Establish Branching Strategy
steps.description: Define a clear workflow where new features are isolated from the production-ready main branch to prevent accidental deployments of broken code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a gitflow configuration script that automates branch creation for features, fixes, and production releases.

steps.title: Automate Documentation with Claude
steps.description: Use AI to generate meaningful commit messages and changelogs automatically, so your version history is always readable for you and your clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current diff and generate a concise, professional commit message following conventional commits specification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message "feat: implement secure oauth flow"

steps.title: Deploy via Version-Linked Pipeline
steps.description: Connect your repository to a CI/CD provider so that every merge to 'main' triggers a clean, automated build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file that builds the project and runs integration tests on every pull request.

pro_tips: Always use 'conventional commits' (e.g., feat:, fix:, chore:) to make your version history searchable and professional for client audits.
Never store API keys in your repo; use a .env.example file in your starter kit to force environment variable discipline.
Run your linter as a pre-commit hook to ensure that no 'dirty' code ever enters your version history.
Use branching to demo features to clients; give them access to a 'staging' branch link rather than local screenshots.
Keep your commits atomic—one logical change per commit makes rolling back specific bugs trivial.
content.next_steps: Once you have your starter kit running with version control, focus on setting up a staging environment. This allows you to show clients progress without breaking their live production site.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your own tools and start shipping features that actually scale. By embedding version control into your workflow, you stop being a developer who 'just writes code' and start being an engineer who delivers reliable, professional SaaS products. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
